spiritual-cramp.d: San Francisco punks with Clash-style swagger; the razor-sharp first pit of the weekend.

vince-staples.d: Long Beach rap's sharpest wit; deadpan bars over sleek West Coast bounce.

four-tet.d: Kieran Hebden's blissed-out, genre-melting electronica; a master of the festival build.

skrillex.d: Bass-music titan headlining Thursday; expect a relentless, surprise-stuffed rave.

villanelle.d: Gene Gallagher (Liam's son) fronts this London trio reviving 90s grunge snarl.

amble.d: Irish folk trio whose hushed three-part harmonies became a word-of-mouth phenomenon.

blues-traveler.d: John Popper's harmonica-led 90s jam vets; 'Run-Around' at golden hour.

yungblud.d: UK rock firebrand with arena-size hooks and chaotic, crowd-hugging energy.

griz.d: Sax-wielding electro-soul party-starter; pure feel-good funk and bass.

the-strokes.d: NYC garage-rock icons headlining Friday with era-defining anthems.

lambrini-girls.d: Brighton's confrontational queer-punk duo; the rowdiest, funniest pit on Friday.

wolfmother.d: Aussie riff-lords; 'Joker and the Thief' is pure Sabbath-sized catharsis live.

zack-fox.d: Comedian-rapper menace; equal parts standup, trolling, and genuine bangers.

wet-leg.d: Isle of Wight deadpan indie stars; 'Chaise Longue' to 'catch these fists', all teeth.

jessie-murph.d: Alabama's smoky-voiced country-pop disruptor, riding 'Blue Strips' to superstardom.

mt-joy.d: Philly indie-folk warmth; sunset singalongs built for the Which Stage.

turnstile.d: Baltimore hardcore turned ecstatic stage-dive communion; an unmissable late-night set.

pawpaw-rod.d: Smooth-talking soul-funk rapper; lowrider grooves for an easy afternoon.

goldie-boutilier.d: Nova Scotia chanteuse making cinematic femme-fatale pop.

rachel-chinouriri.d: British indie-pop riser with diaristic heartbreak anthems like 'All I Ever Asked'.

mother-mother.d: Canadian cult quintet whose quirky harmonies conquered the internet ('Hayloft').

smino.d: St. Louis' silkiest rapper-singer; funk-soaked flows and featherlight falsetto.

hot-mulligan.d: Michigan emo loudmouths; scream-along catharsis and absurd song titles.

the-dare.d: NYC's suited sleaze-pop provocateur ('Girls'); a sweaty 1:30 a.m. dance riot.

dora-jar.d: Whimsical alt-pop songwriter beloved by Billie Eilish; intricate, strange, charming.

wednesday.d: Asheville's countrygaze heroes: pedal steel, shredding, and Karly Hartzman's gut-punch stories.

the-chats.d: Australian shed-rock larrikins; two-minute pub-punk ragers ('Smoko').

bbno.d: Vancouver's goofiest hitmaker ('edamame'); irony-laced bangers, maximum fun.

geese.d: Brooklyn's wildest young art-rock band; 'Getting Killed' made them critics' darlings.

blood-orange.d: Dev Hynes' luxurious art-pop and R&B; lush, melancholy grooves.

lil-jon.d: YEAH! The king of crunk turns That Tent into a midnight party time machine.

jackie-hollander.d: Indie-dance producer and vocalist; hazy, hooky house cuts like "Addicted".

daniel-allan.d: Rising producer blending indie vocals with glossy dance-pop drops.

aszewo.d: LA trio Łaszewo blends breezy indie vocals with house grooves; 'messy' is the vibe.

notion.d: UK bass producer with garage-flavored heaters like 'Hooked'; millions of monthly listeners.

adventure-club.d: Canadian melodic-dubstep veterans; emotional drops and throwback feels.

sidepiece.d: Grammy-nominated duo (Party Favor x Nitti Gritti); 'On My Mind' groove machine.

cloonee.d: UK tech-house heavyweight and Hellbent label boss; relentless club heaters.

major-lazer.d: Diplo's dancehall-EDM crew; nonstop hits and beautiful chaos at 1:30 a.m.

inzo-sunrise-set.d: 'Overthinker' producer INZO guides a psychedelic sunrise comedown.

probcause.d: Chicago rapper-producer fusing hip-hop and bass; longtime GRiZ collaborator.

mary-droppinz.d: LA selector dropping gritty house and bass for the late-late crew.

richard-finger.d: Anonymous duo making 'unreasonably heavy' dubstep; a SoundCloud cult hit turned Red Rocks act.

eazybaked.d: Anonymous experimental-bass duo; glitchy, face-melting weirdness at 4 a.m.

lumasi.d: Rising bass producer with deep, meditative wubs to close out the Where Stage.

midnight-generation.d: Mexican disco-funk band led by Fernando Mares; viral 'Young Girl' grooves.

arcy-drive.d: Long Island 'attic rock' band; scrappy, feel-good indie anthems.

tash-sultana.d: One-person band looping guitar, keys and sax into hypnotic psych-soul jams.

alabama-shakes.d: Brittany Howard's reunited Southern soul-rock powerhouse; 'Hold On' hits harder live.

the-neighbourhood.d: Dark SoCal alt-rock, back from hiatus; 'Sweater Weather' under Saturday lights.

r-f-s-du-sol.d: Australian live-electronic trio headlining Saturday; 'Innerbloom' on the farm at midnight.

steph-strings.d: Melbourne percussive-guitar virtuoso; sunny, jaw-dropping instrumental folk.

mountain-grass-unit.d: Birmingham's young jamgrass pickers; also guesting in Kesha's SuperJam.

holly-humberstone.d: British songwriter of aching, intimate alt-pop confessionals.

amyl-and-the-sniffers.d: Amy Taylor's Aussie pub-punk wrecking crew; feral, hilarious, unmissable.

rainbow-kitten-surprise.d: Genre-blurring NC favorites; 'Cocaine Jesus' singalongs and a SuperJam cameo.

teddy-swims.d: The tattooed voice behind 'Lose Control'; one of pop-soul's biggest belters.

weird-al-yankovic-bigger-weirder-late-ni.d: The parody legend goes Bigger & Weirder for a one-of-a-kind late-night Rooview.

sunami.d: Bay Area beatdown hardcore; a 12:45 p.m. mosh-call alarm clock.

congress-the-band.d: Rising rock-and-soul outfit bringing warm, retro grooves to This Tent.

waylon-wyatt.d: Arkansas teen songwriter gone viral; raw, heartfelt country in the Zach Bryan lineage.

the-runarounds.d: Wilmington band born on the Prime Video series—now a real-deal touring rock act.

passion-pit.d: Michael Angelakos' euphoric synth-pop; 'Sleepyhead' nostalgia in This Tent.

kesha-presents-superjam-the-alchemy-of-p.d: Kesha's all-star pop ritual: Chromeo, Grouplove, Margo Price, RKS, Wyatt Flores, Blondshell & more.

osees.d: John Dwyer's hyper-prolific garage-psych machine; two drummers, zero letup.

snow-strippers.d: Detroit duo's lo-fi electroclash; the internet's favorite after-dark act.

the-stews.d: Southern garage rockers with beachy riffs and festival-honed live energy.

confidence-man.d: Aussie dance-pop absurdists; choreography, costumes, and pure camp euphoria.

trixie-mattel.d: Drag superstar spinning a high-camp party set—expect hits, gags, and glamour.

wyatt-flores.d: Oklahoma red-dirt phenom; tearjerker anthems plus a SuperJam guest spot.

sg-lewis.d: UK producer of glossy disco-house; nonstop feel-good grooves.

flipturn.d: Florida indie rockers; 'August' euphoria built for the evening slot.

freddie-gibbs-the-alchemist.d: Gangsta Gibbs and Uncle Al run back Alfredo: coke-rap poetry over velvet loops.

nikita-the-wicked.d: Theatrical dark-bass riser; commanding vocals over wicked drops.

juelz.d: Vancouver-bred Sable Valley star; emotional trap anthems with hard-hitting drops.

deathpact.d: Masked mystery project delivering sinister mid-tempo and surgical bass.

boys-noize.d: Berlin electro-techno icon; grimy warehouse energy all night.

sub-focus.d: UK D&B heavyweight; euphoric, high-velocity festival drum & bass.

sara-landry.d: The high priestess of hard techno; pummeling industrial kicks.

chase-status.d: UK D&B institution; 'Baddadan' chaos after midnight.

gorgon-city-sunrise-set.d: UK deep-house duo Gorgon City steering a 3-hour marathon from 2 a.m. to sunrise.

steve-cory-are-dead.d: Mystery billing on the Where Stage—show up and find out who Steve and Cory are.

costa.d: Viral flipper CØSTA, whose 'Bass Cannon' and Timbaland edits broke dubstep TikTok.

clozee.d: French world-bass voyager; lush global textures, also half of LSZEE.

big-gigantic.d: Sax-and-drums livetronica vets playing twice: a woods sunrise set and a Sunday banger.

smoakland.d: Oakland bass duo; heavyweight wubs engineered for the woods.

effin.d: New Jersey dubstep riser with filthy, technical sound design.

aly-aj.d: Disney-era icons turned synth-pop sophisticates; 'Potential Breakup Song' forever.

trombone-shorty.d: New Orleans brass dynamo; a horn-fueled Sunday funk celebration.

tedeschi-trucks-band.d: Susan and Derek's 12-piece blues-rock revue; the farm's finest musicianship.

role-model.d: Tucker Pillsbury's heartthrob pop; 'Sally, When the Wine Runs Out' dance party.

noah-kahan.d: Vermont's stick-season superstar closes the fest with shout-along folk-pop catharsis.

little-stranger.d: Charleston alt-hip-hop duo; breezy, funny beach-rap grooves.

spacey-jane.d: Perth indie rockers; 'Booster Seat' melancholy wrapped in jangly hooks.

japanese-breakfast.d: Michelle Zauner's shimmering art-pop; joy, grief, and one triumphant gong.

clipse.d: Pusha T and Malice, reunited and razor-sharp; precision luxury rap, brick by brick.

kesha.d: Pop's glitter-punk survivor with wall-to-wall hits, fresh off Saturday's SuperJam.

nat-myers.d: Korean-American blues howler reviving 1930s bottleneck stomp.

buffalo-traffic-jam.d: Bozeman, Montana folk duo gone supernova; intimate harmonies, sold-out tours.

hemlocke-springs.d: The 'girlfriend' viral star; giddy, off-kilter 80s synth-pop with zero posing.

fcukers.d: NYC indie-dance trio; 'Bon Bon' turned every club into their house party.

turnover.d: Virginia Beach emo-turned-dream-pop romantics; 'Peripheral Vision' favorites.

modest-mouse.d: Isaac Brock's indie-rock institution; 'Float On' plus decades of jagged classics.

soupless.d: Nashville roommate duo making fuzzy, pretty-guitar indie pop; an early-bird gem.

girl-tones.d: Bowling Green sister duo; classically trained, volcano-loud garage rock.

blondshell.d: Sabrina Teitelbaum's 90s-leaning alt-rock; razor lyrics, huge choruses, SuperJam guest.

audrey-hobert.d: The 'Sue Me' singer (and Gracie Abrams co-writer) stepping into her own spotlight.

del-water-gap.d: S. Holden Jaffe's heart-on-sleeve indie pop; also moonlighting in the SuperJam.

mariah-the-scientist.d: Atlanta R&B romantic; slow-burning, starry-eyed ballads like 'Burning Blue'.

motifv.d: Dreamy, starlit melodic bass from producer Motifv.

a-hundred-drums.d: Gabrielle Watson's tribal, meditative deep-bass shamanism.

san-holo.d: Dutch future-bass auteur of 'Light'; guitar-laced euphoria via his bitbird label.

daily-bread.d: Atlanta's electro-soul beatsmith; dusty soul samples over fat bass.

lszee.d: CloZee x LSDREAM joint project; double the cosmic bass dreamscape.
